This family consists of approximately 2,000 species, which are commonly known as pipeworts or erio-caulids. The genus Paepalanthus is a part of this family and contains over 200 species. intro: Paepalanthus nitens, also known as "M'borogo," is a plant species belonging to the family Eriocaulaceae, native to Africa and used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Paepalanthus nitens include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Paepalanthus nitens include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are traditionally used in African folk medicine to alleviate various health issues.
